prompt: A minimalist, single-frame data visualization of Arctic sea ice decline. The composition is a vertical stack of geometric circular forms against a dark, moody background. Top forms are large, thick, solid white discs representing early years. Moving downward, the forms become progressively smaller, thinner, and more fragmented into simple, clean, almost cartoonish ice floe shapes. The lowest forms are tiny, isolated fragments reflecting in a dark, rippled pool at the base. The background is a vertical gradient from deep charcoal at the top to a hostile, dark amber-red glow at the bottom. Style is severe, editorial, and clean. Negative prompt: text, numbers, labels, legend, axes, chart, infographic, people, polar bears, globes, thermometers, glossy sci-fi, realistic 3D textures.
insight: The systematic conversion of a chronological time series into a physical sequence of diminishing, fragmenting ice forms to visualize the inevitable loss of Arctic sea ice.
